steps.title: Find the argument's link
steps.content: Parker and Anagnostou infer local cultural continuity from stable obsidian-object production methods at Malia. The architectural transition alone is not enough; their argument depends on treating production methods as a cultural marker. To weaken that argument, a finding must show that the same methods were not unique to the local culture.

steps.title: Break the uniqueness claim
steps.content: A addresses the obsidian source, not production methods. C may even support continuity by showing Malia kept a different method. D says building changes need not affect obsidian methods. Shared methods across cultures weaken the inference by giving another reason the same technique could persist. The answer is B.
